frame_analyses.description: The needle is being loaded into the needle holder.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle is aligned and gripped close to the swage end for control.

frame_analyses.description: Beginning to place suture through the marked sites on the drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Care taken to align needle with marked points on the drain.

frame_analyses.description: First throw of the knot being made, surgeon's knot expected.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot with double throw is being tied.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being executed, hand switching is applicable.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch occurred correctly while maintaining tension.

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being completed with alternating hand.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent hand switch and effective knot tensioning observed.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting the suture ends to complete the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture ends cut cleanly within the training box.

knot_assessments.notes: Well-executed surgeon's knot with adequate tension.
Single throw with effective hand switch.
Final single throw maintains knot integrity.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit resists separation after knot tying.

technique_summary: The participant demonstrated good control during suture placement and knot tying. Hand switches were performed correctly and the suturing resulted in a stable, well-tensioned knot. Minimal suture deviation noted.
improvement_suggestions: Pay closer attention to suture alignment with marks to reduce deviation.
Aim for more efficient instrument movements to improve completion time.
strengths: Excellent hand coordination during knot tying.
Consistently maintained knot tension throughout the throws.
